dashboard.label: Overview
dashboard.definition:
{"title":"Overview","description":"Wearables platform overview","visualizations":{"ov_md":{"type":"splunk.markdown","options":{"markdown":"# Wearables Health

Vendor-neutral health dashboards built on the **Wearables data model** — one set
of panels that work across devices (Oura today; Garmin and others later).

## How it fits together
- **Per-vendor add-ons** (`TA-oura`, `TA-garmin`, …) ingest each device's data and
 normalize it into canonical fields (e.g. `total_sleep_min`, `avg_hr`, `steps`).
- **Wearables data model** (Sleep / Heart Rate / Activity) exposes those canonical
 fields, tagged `wearable_sleep` / `wearable_heartrate` / `wearable_activity`.
- **These dashboards** query the model with a `vendor` / `person` filter, plus
 vendor-specific panels for proprietary metrics (Oura Readiness, Garmin Body Battery).

## Access & identity
- Data carries an indexed **`person_id`** (opaque) + **`vendor`**, stamped at ingest.
- Per-person isolation is enforced by role `srchFilter` on `person_id` (RBAC).
- `person_id → name/goals` comes from an admin-managed enrichment lookup.

## Status
Scaffold — the data model is defined; dashboards are migrating from the
single-vendor **oura_health** app (which remains the GA fallback). Requires
`index=wearables`, the `TA-oura` add-on, and person_id-based RBAC.

## Source
[github.com/narwhaldc](https://github.com/narwhaldc)
","backgroundColor":"rgba(15,17,23,0.50)"}}},"dataSources":{},"inputs":{},"layout":{"type":"absolute","options":{"width":1440,"height":760,"display":"auto"},"structure":[{"item":"ov_md","type":"block","position":{"x":20,"y":20,"w":1400,"h":720}}],"globalInputs":[]}}
